section_label: NICU priority batch — 40 drugs (antibiotics, cardiovascular, sedation, surfactant, critical care)
review_disclaimer: DRAFT — pending clinician review. Verify every dose against your local protocol and current vial concentration before administration. PediAid assumes no liability.

india_formulations.notes: Oral used for chronic suppression; primary NICU therapy is IV.

doses.duration: 21 days for CNS/disseminated; 14 days for SEM (skin-eye-mouth)
doses.comments: Continue IV until repeat CSF HSV PCR negative for CNS disease.

doses.indication: HSV chronic suppression after IV course

drugs.monitoring: Renal function (Cr, urine output) at baseline + weekly; CBC; LFTs. Adequate hydration during infusion to prevent crystal nephropathy.
drugs.adverse_effects: Crystal nephropathy (avoid by hydration + slow infusion), neutropenia, transaminitis, phlebitis, lethargy/tremor/seizure (high doses or accumulation in renal impairment).
drugs.renal_adjustment: Reduce frequency in renal impairment; monitor creatinine. KCNT for renal failure: see NEJM CASG 2011.
drugs.incompatibilities: Not with other meds in same line — phlebitis risk; pH ~11.

cross_checks.agreement: agrees on 20 mg/kg q8h CNS HSV term

doses.route: IV (rapid push, central or large peripheral vein, follow with NS flush)
doses.frequency: Single push; assess for conversion within 1–2 min
doses.comments: Push as fast as possible — half-life ~10 sec.

doses.indication: PSVT — repeat doses if first does not convert
doses.max_per_dose: 0.3 mg/kg/dose or 12 mg/dose, whichever is lower

drugs.monitoring: Continuous ECG + BP; have crash cart and pacing available.
drugs.adverse_effects: Brief asystole, flushing, dyspnoea, chest tightness, bronchospasm. Effects last seconds.
drugs.contraindications: 2nd/3rd-degree AV block or sick sinus syndrome (without pacemaker); known hypersensitivity.
drugs.incompatibilities: Theophylline / caffeine block adenosine receptors — may reduce efficacy.

cross_checks.agreement: agrees on 0.1 mg/kg → 0.2 mg/kg, max 6/12 mg

review.notes: Most NICU brochures cite max 6 mg/12 mg (PALS); Neofax cites 0.3 mg/kg or 12 mg per dose. Use the lower of the two.

doses.indication: Maintain ductal patency in ductal-dependent congenital heart disease
doses.route: IV continuous infusion (UAC tip near ductus also acceptable)
doses.dose_per_kg_per_min_range: 0.01–0.1 mcg/kg/min (titrate to oxygenation vs adverse effects)
doses.comments: Maintenance can be as low as 0.01 mcg/kg/min. Higher initial doses are NOT more effective and have higher AE rates.

drugs.monitoring: Continuous SpO₂ + HR + BP + RR; apnoea monitor; temperature.
drugs.adverse_effects: Apnoea (10–12% of treated neonates, especially < 2 kg, usually within first hour) — be ready for intubation. Fever, hypotension, flushing, jitteriness, diarrhoea. Long-term: cortical hyperostosis, antral hyperplasia.
drugs.contraindications: Respiratory distress syndrome where PGE1 worsens hypoxaemia (rare); known hypersensitivity.
drugs.black_box_warning: Apnoea — risk in 10–12% of treated neonates with CHD, especially < 2 kg birth weight, usually within first hour of infusion. Have ventilation support immediately available.

review.notes: Always intubate / prepare to intubate before transport on PGE1.

doses.comments: Source: Smits / Rivera-Chaparro 2017. Target peak > 24 mg/L, trough < 3 mg/L.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 14 mg/kg q48h / 12 mg/kg q36h / continued q36h

drugs.monitoring: Therapeutic levels — Peak (1 h post infusion) > 24 mg/L (target 25–35); Trough (just pre-next-dose) < 3 mg/L. Audiology screen at discharge for any > 5-day exposure.
drugs.adverse_effects: Nephrotoxicity, ototoxicity (often irreversible — vestibular and auditory), neuromuscular blockade in concurrent use of Mg/curariform agents.
drugs.contraindications: Aminoglycoside hypersensitivity; severe renal impairment without level monitoring.
drugs.black_box_warning: Aminoglycoside therapy: neurotoxicity, ototoxicity, nephrotoxicity. Risk increased with renal impairment, dehydration, prolonged therapy. Discontinue or adjust if ototoxicity or nephrotoxicity appears. Often irreversible.
drugs.incompatibilities: Do NOT co-infuse with β-lactams (ampicillin / penicillin G inactivate aminoglycosides in vitro).

cross_checks.agreement: primary source for the weight × age table

review.notes: Some Indian units use 15 mg/kg once daily for term neonates; verify local protocol.

doses.indication: Refractory VT / VF / SVT — IV loading
doses.comments: Correct K, Mg, Ca before initiating. May repeat × 1–2 doses up to total 15 mg/kg.
Concentration ≤ 2 mg/mL via peripheral; > 2 mg/mL only via central line. Switch to oral within 24–48 h.

drugs.monitoring: Continuous ECG (QTc, AV block); BP during loading; LFTs, TFTs at baseline + serially; pulmonary review.
drugs.adverse_effects: Pulmonary toxicity (interstitial pneumonitis), hepatotoxicity, hypothyroidism / hyperthyroidism, blue-grey skin discolouration, corneal microdeposits, photosensitivity. IV: hypotension, bradycardia.
drugs.contraindications: Cardiogenic shock, sick sinus syndrome / 2nd–3rd degree AV block (without pacemaker), severe sinus bradycardia, iodine allergy.
drugs.black_box_warning: Pulmonary, hepatic, and cardiac toxicity. Use only for life-threatening arrhythmias — substantial toxicity. May proarrhythmia (QT prolongation, torsades).

cross_checks.agreement: agrees on 5 mg/kg IV load over 20–60 min

review.notes: Avoid undiluted bolus in pulseless VT/VF — give over 1–2 min for cardiac arrest only.

india_formulations.notes: Oral atropine rarely used in NICU.

doses.route: IV (over 1 min, immediately before other premeds)

doses.loading_dose_per_kg: 0.02 mg/kg, doubled every 5 min until atropinisation
doses.dose_per_kg_per_min_range: Maintenance infusion 10–20% of total loading dose / hr (max 2 mg/hr)

drugs.monitoring: HR, BP, pupils, mucous membranes (dry), bowel sounds.
drugs.adverse_effects: Tachycardia, mydriasis, dry mucous membranes, urinary retention, fever, paradoxical bradycardia at very low doses (theoretical in neonates — rarely clinically significant).

cross_checks.agreement: agrees on 0.02 mg/kg IV/IO, max 0.5 mg single dose

review.notes: Many guidelines recommend min single dose 0.1 mg to avoid theoretical paradoxical bradycardia; data in true neonates are limited.

doses.route: Intratracheal (4 quarter-dose aliquots in different positions)
doses.frequency: First dose within 15 min of birth; up to 4 doses in first 48 h, no more frequently than every 6 h
First dose by 8 h of age; up to 4 doses in first 48 h, no more frequently than every 6 h
doses.comments: Confirm RDS on chest X-ray before subsequent doses.

drugs.administration: Warm vial to room temperature (do NOT shake). Suction ETT before. Disconnect from ventilator briefly between aliquots. Reconnect, hand-bag for 15–30 sec. Re-position infant for next aliquot. Do not suction for at least 1 hour after dosing unless airway obstruction.
drugs.monitoring: Continuous SpO₂ + HR + chest expansion during instillation. Watch for transient bradycardia / desaturation, ETT obstruction, pneumothorax.
drugs.adverse_effects: Transient bradycardia, desaturation, reflux up the ETT, pulmonary haemorrhage (rare), air leak (PIE, pneumothorax).

review.notes: Indian NICUs increasingly use poractant alfa (Curosurf) due to evidence of lower mortality at 200 mg/kg. Verify local availability.

india_formulations.strength: Various — typically 100 mg/5 mL elemental calcium
india_formulations.notes: 10 % calcium gluconate IV: 100 mg/mL salt = 9.3 mg/mL elemental Ca = 0.465 mEq/mL.

doses.route: IV slow over 10–30 min (NEVER IM/SC — extravasation causes necrosis)
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 10–20 mg/kg elemental Ca (= 100–200 mg/kg/dose calcium gluconate)
doses.frequency: Single dose; follow with continuous infusion if persistent

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 50–80 mg/kg/day elemental Ca (= 538–860 mg/kg/day calcium gluconate)
doses.frequency: Continuous IV for 48 h, then taper; or oral in 3–4 divided doses
Repeat as needed for ECG changes

drugs.administration: Slow infusion only (max 50 mg/min elemental Ca = 500 mg/min gluconate). Confirm IV patency before each dose — extravasation causes severe necrosis.
drugs.monitoring: ECG (look for short QT, ST depression in hypercalcaemia), serum ionised Ca, IV site for infiltration.
drugs.adverse_effects: Bradycardia, hypotension if given too fast. Tissue necrosis on extravasation. Vomiting, flushing, tingling.
drugs.incompatibilities: Do NOT mix with NaHCO₃ or phosphate-containing solutions — precipitate.

cross_checks.agreement: agrees on 100–200 mg/kg/dose for symptomatic hypoCa

review.notes: Always document whether dose is in mg of CALCIUM GLUCONATE (salt) or mg of ELEMENTAL CALCIUM — 10× difference.

doses.frequency: Up to 3 doses every 12 h (or every 6 h in trial protocols if persistently intubated and FiO₂ ≥ 30%)
doses.comments: Prophylaxis: within 30 min of birth; rescue: within 2 h.

drugs.monitoring: Same as beractant — SpO₂, HR, chest expansion, watch for transient bradycardia/desaturation.
drugs.adverse_effects: Transient bradycardia, desaturation, reflux up ETT, rare pulmonary haemorrhage, air leak.

review.notes: Calfactant rarely available in India — poractant alfa (Curosurf) is the local primary surfactant.

doses.indication: Skin / soft tissue / bone-joint / surgical prophylaxis

drugs.monitoring: Clinical response; CBC, RFT for prolonged use.
drugs.adverse_effects: Rash, eosinophilia, transaminitis, rare neutropenia, phlebitis.

drugs.monitoring: Clinical response; CBC, RFT; neuro status (cefepime-induced encephalopathy in renal impairment).
drugs.adverse_effects: Rash, eosinophilia, transaminitis, neutropenia. Cefepime-induced encephalopathy / NCSE in renal impairment — caution in oliguric infants.

doses.comments: Smaller doses / longer intervals for VLBW < 2 kg.

drugs.monitoring: Clinical response; CBC, RFT; LFTs.
drugs.adverse_effects: Rash, eosinophilia, transaminitis, neutropenia, phlebitis.
drugs.incompatibilities: Do not mix with sodium bicarbonate, aminoglycosides in the same line.

review.notes: Often supply-constrained globally — many units now use cefepime instead.

drugs.monitoring: Clinical response; CBC, RFT.
drugs.adverse_effects: Rash, eosinophilia, transaminitis, neutropenia.

drugs.monitoring: Bilirubin (caution in jaundice — displaces from albumin); clinical response.
drugs.adverse_effects: Diarrhoea, biliary pseudolithiasis, displacement of bilirubin from albumin → kernicterus risk in neonates, especially preterm.
drugs.contraindications: PRETERM NEONATES — contraindicated. Hyperbilirubinaemia. Concurrent administration with IV calcium-containing solutions (including PN) in any neonate — fatal Ca-ceftriaxone precipitates have been reported.
drugs.black_box_warning: Contraindicated in premature neonates and any neonate with hyperbilirubinaemia. Concurrent use with IV calcium-containing solutions in any neonate is contraindicated — fatal precipitation in lung and kidney.

review.notes: AVOID in any neonate < 28 days if calcium-containing PN is running — switch to cefotaxime or cefepime.

drugs.monitoring: Stool pattern (C. diff colitis), LFTs on prolonged use.
drugs.adverse_effects: Diarrhoea, C. difficile colitis (black-box class warning), rash, transaminitis, rare blood dyscrasias.
drugs.black_box_warning: Clostridioides difficile-associated diarrhoea (CDAD) reported with virtually all antibacterials, including clindamycin — may range from mild diarrhoea to fatal colitis.

india_formulations.notes: 200 mg/kg buccal massage (≥ 35 wk and ≤ 48 h old) for transient hypoglycaemia.

doses.frequency: May repeat × up to 6 doses over 48 h

doses.comments: Titrate to maintain blood glucose in target range. Do not stop abruptly — rebound hypoglycaemia.

drugs.monitoring: Capillary blood glucose, urine glucose if GIR > 12 mg/kg/min.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hyperglycaemia, osmotic diuresis, hyperosmolar dehydration, IV irritation (concentrations > 12.5 % via central line only).
drugs.incompatibilities: 10 % is peripherally tolerated; ≥ 12.5 % needs central access.

review.notes: GIR mg/kg/min = (% dex × rate mL/hr × 0.167) / wt(kg). PediAid has a GIR calculator.

doses.comments: Begin at low dose; titrate. Useful when myocardial dysfunction with preserved BP.

drugs.monitoring: HR, BP, ECG, urine output, lactate.
drugs.adverse_effects: Tachycardia, ectopy, mild hypotension at higher doses, increased myocardial O₂ demand, hypokalaemia.
drugs.incompatibilities: Do NOT mix in same line as alkaline solutions (NaHCO₃) — degrades.

review.notes: PediAid Emergency NICU/PICU drug calculator handles dilution prep.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_min_range: 2–20 mcg/kg/min ; for fluid-refractory shock start < 10 mcg/kg/min
doses.comments: Dose-dependent: low (2–5 mcg/kg/min) — renal/dopaminergic ; medium (5–10) — β1 inotrope ; high (> 10) — α1 vasoconstrictor.

drugs.monitoring: HR, BP (continuous if available), ECG, urine output, perfusion, IV site for infiltration.
drugs.adverse_effects: Tachyarrhythmia, hypertension, peripheral / mesenteric / renal vasoconstriction at high doses, IV infiltration → tissue necrosis.
drugs.black_box_warning: Tissue sloughing on IV infiltration — infiltrate area with phentolamine 1 mg/mL (1–5 mL).
drugs.incompatibilities: Do NOT mix with alkaline solutions; ampicillin; sodium bicarbonate.

doses.comments: Tolerance develops within days.
Rapid push > 5 mcg/kg may cause chest-wall rigidity — paralysis required.

drugs.monitoring: Continuous HR, RR, SpO₂; pain/sedation score; bowel sounds.
drugs.adverse_effects: Respiratory depression, chest-wall rigidity (rapid push), hypotension, bradycardia, urinary retention, ileus, tolerance, neonatal abstinence on weaning > 5 days.
drugs.contraindications: Severe respiratory depression without ventilation support.
drugs.black_box_warning: Risk of addiction, abuse, misuse. Life-threatening respiratory depression. Concomitant benzodiazepines or CNS depressants → profound sedation, respiratory depression, coma, death.

review.notes: Always have naloxone available bedside.

doses.duration: 2 weeks after blood culture clearance and symptom resolution

doses.indication: Invasive candidiasis prophylaxis (BW < 1000 g, or < 1500 g in high-incidence units)

drugs.monitoring: LFTs, RFT.
drugs.adverse_effects: Transaminitis, GI upset, rash, rare QT prolongation.
drugs.contraindications: Concurrent QT-prolonging drugs (relative); known azole hypersensitivity.

review.notes: Higher dose (12–25 mg/kg load) used in critically ill / ECMO.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 1 mg/kg/dose initial; may increase to 2 mg/kg IV or 6 mg/kg PO

doses.frequency: every 12 hours initially (every 6–8 h if > 1 month)
doses.comments: Consider alternate-day therapy for long-term use.

drugs.monitoring: Electrolytes (Na, K, Cl, HCO₃), BUN/Cr, weight, urine output, hearing if prolonged or in renal impairment.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypokalaemia, hyponatraemia, hypochloraemic alkalosis, hypocalcaemia, hypomagnesaemia, ototoxicity (high dose, fast push), nephrocalcinosis (with prolonged therapy), PDA.
drugs.black_box_warning: Profound diuresis with water + electrolyte depletion if excessive doses — careful clinical supervision required.

review.notes: Consider furosemide infusion 0.05–0.4 mg/kg/h for refractory volume overload.

doses.comments: A second course may be given.

drugs.monitoring: Renal function (Cr, urine output), platelets, GI bleeding signs.
drugs.adverse_effects: Renal impairment, oliguria, GI perforation, NEC, IVH (less than indomethacin), platelet dysfunction.
drugs.contraindications: Active bleeding, NEC, severe oliguria, thrombocytopenia (< 50k), hyperbilirubinaemia (relative — displaces from albumin).
drugs.black_box_warning: Cardiovascular thrombotic events, MI, stroke (NSAID class warning) — primarily applies to chronic adult use, not the short PDA course.

review.notes: Indomethacin and paracetamol (acetaminophen) are alternatives; ibuprofen has lower NEC and renal AE rates than indomethacin.

doses.comments: Hold subsequent doses if anuria / severe oliguria / Cr rising.

doses.frequency: every 24 hours × 3 doses, started within 6–24 h of birth

drugs.monitoring: Renal function, urine output, platelets, ductal status (echo), bleeding, GI status.
drugs.adverse_effects: Renal impairment / oliguria (often transient), GI perforation, NEC, IVH, platelet dysfunction.
drugs.contraindications: Active bleeding, NEC, severe oliguria, thrombocytopenia, hyperbilirubinaemia (relative).
drugs.black_box_warning: NSAID class — CV thrombotic events; contraindicated in CABG. Renal effects in neonates can be substantial.

review.notes: Indian NICUs increasingly prefer ibuprofen or paracetamol over indomethacin due to lower NEC rates.

doses.comments: Use 1 U/mL or dilute as 0.1 U/mL for very low rates. Always run via a syringe pump.
Monitor glucose hourly; hypoglycaemia is the chief risk.
Basal 0.14–0.7 U/kg/day; titrate to capillary glucose.

drugs.monitoring: Hourly glucose during infusion changes; serum K (drops fast).
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypoglycaemia (life-threatening), hypokalaemia.
drugs.incompatibilities: Insulin adsorbs to plastic tubing — flush 20 mL of insulin solution through new tubing before connecting to the patient.

review.notes: Always specify 'human regular insulin' (not analogue) for neonatal IV use.

drugs.monitoring: CBC weekly (myelosuppression), lactate (if metabolic acidosis suspected), neuro status (peripheral / optic neuropathy with prolonged use).
drugs.adverse_effects: Myelosuppression (especially > 14 days — thrombocytopenia, anaemia), serotonin syndrome with serotonergic drugs, lactic acidosis, peripheral / optic neuropathy.
drugs.contraindications: MAOI use (within 2 weeks); concomitant serotonergic drugs (relative).

review.notes: Limit to MRSA / VRE infection where standard β-lactams or vancomycin can't be used.

drugs.monitoring: RR, SpO₂, HR (respiratory depression), level of consciousness.
drugs.adverse_effects: Respiratory depression, hypotension, paradoxical agitation, sedation.
drugs.contraindications: Acute narrow-angle glaucoma; severe respiratory insufficiency without ventilation; sleep apnoea.
drugs.black_box_warning: Concomitant use with opioids → profound sedation, respiratory depression, coma, death. Risk of dependence and withdrawal.

review.notes: Injection contains benzyl alcohol — caution in preterm < 1500 g (gasping syndrome).

doses.route: IV / IO rapid push (over several minutes)

drugs.monitoring: ECG (QT, AV block), DTRs, RR, BP, serum Mg, calcium, renal function.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypotension, bradycardia, respiratory depression, hyporeflexia, hypocalcaemia.
drugs.contraindications: Heart block, severe renal impairment without level monitoring.

review.notes: 1 g MgSO₄ = ~8 mEq Mg = ~98 mg elemental Mg. Always document salt vs elemental.

doses.frequency: every 8–12 hours by GA / postnatal age band

drugs.monitoring: Clinical response; CBC, RFT, LFTs.
drugs.adverse_effects: Diarrhoea, eosinophilia, transaminitis, rare seizures (especially in renal impairment), thrombocytosis.

review.notes: Reserve for ESBL or critically ill MDR sepsis; use stewardship.

india_formulations.strength: 2 mg/mL (Versed brand abroad; rare in India)

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: IV 0.05–0.15 mg/kg ; IN 0.2–0.3 mg/kg ; SL 0.2 mg/kg ; PO 0.25 mg/kg

doses.comments: Tolerance with prolonged use.

drugs.monitoring: RR, SpO₂, HR, BP, sedation level.
drugs.adverse_effects: Respiratory depression, hypotension (especially with opioids or rapid push), paradoxical agitation, withdrawal after prolonged use.
drugs.contraindications: Acute narrow-angle glaucoma; KIDs list — avoid in VLBW due to IVH/PVL/death risk.
drugs.black_box_warning: Respiratory depression / arrest — only use where continuous monitoring is available.

review.notes: Avoid in VLBW unless absolutely necessary; prefer fentanyl + dexmedetomidine or morphine in ELBW.

doses.indication: Low cardiac output (post-cardiac surgery, PPHN, septic shock)
doses.loading_dose_per_kg: 50 mcg/kg over 15 min (or 75 mcg/kg over 60 min)
doses.comments: In premature < 30 wk GA, infuse load over 3 hours. Adjust by haemodynamic response.

drugs.monitoring: Continuous BP, HR, ECG, urine output, electrolytes (K, Mg).
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypotension (especially during load — pretreat with volume), arrhythmia, thrombocytopenia.
drugs.incompatibilities: Do NOT mix with furosemide — precipitate.

review.notes: Some neonatal units omit the loading dose to avoid hypotension.

doses.comments: Postoperative may need higher rates.
Wean by 10–20% every 2–3 days based on Finnegan score.
Reduced clearance during cooling — lower doses.

drugs.monitoring: RR, SpO₂, HR, BP, sedation/pain score, bowel sounds.
drugs.adverse_effects: Respiratory depression, hypotension, bradycardia, urinary retention, ileus, pruritus, tolerance, NAS on weaning > 5 days.
drugs.contraindications: Severe respiratory depression without ventilation support; concurrent MAOI (or within 14 days); severe GI obstruction.
drugs.black_box_warning: Respiratory depression — neonates highly sensitive. Interaction with benzodiazepines, alcohol, other CNS depressants → fatal respiratory depression.

review.notes: Always have naloxone bedside. PediAid Drug Calculator handles infusion prep.

doses.comments: ETT dose 2–3× IV dose. WARNING: KIDs list — avoid in postpartum resuscitation due to seizure risk.
Use lower dose to preserve analgesia in iatrogenic over-sedation.

drugs.monitoring: Continuous RR, SpO₂, HR, GCS (re-narcotisation possible — half-life of naloxone often shorter than the opioid).
drugs.adverse_effects: Withdrawal in opioid-tolerant infants (NAS / mother on opioids → seizure, vomiting, pulmonary oedema, cardiac arrest), hypertension, tachycardia.
drugs.contraindications: Maternal opioid use / chronic opioid exposure (relative — use lowest effective dose).

cross_checks.agreement: no longer recommended in delivery room as part of initial resuscitation

review.notes: Naloxone is no longer part of routine NRP in the delivery room — establish ventilation first.

doses.frequency: every 12 h × first 7 days, then every 8 hours

drugs.monitoring: Clinical response; CBC, electrolytes (large volumes of K-penicillin → hyperkalaemia).
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypersensitivity (rare in neonates), seizures at very high CSF doses, eosinophilia, rare interstitial nephritis.

review.notes: USE ONLY aqueous crystalline penicillin G (potassium or sodium) for IV — NOT benzathine or procaine. 1 mg = 1600 IU.

doses.comments: If seizures persist 15 min after first load, give additional 10–20 mg/kg up to total 40 mg/kg.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 1.5 mg/kg/dose every 8 h OR 2.25 mg/kg/dose every 12 h

doses.frequency: Started 12 h after the loading dose

doses.comments: Wean by 20% every other day based on Finnegan score.

drugs.monitoring: Continuous SpO₂, RR (respiratory depression), level of consciousness, drug level (target 20–40 mcg/mL for status epilepticus), LFTs.
drugs.adverse_effects: Respiratory depression, sedation, hypotension, immunosuppression, paradoxical hyperactivity (later).
drugs.contraindications: Severe respiratory depression without ventilation; porphyria.
drugs.black_box_warning: Concomitant use with opioids → profound sedation, respiratory depression, coma, death.

review.notes: First-line for neonatal seizures across most Indian and international guidelines.

doses.infusion_rate: Do not exceed 1–3 mg/kg/min (or 50 mg/min, whichever is slower)
doses.comments: Watch for severe hypotension and arrhythmia.

drugs.monitoring: Continuous ECG (bradycardia, AV block) and BP during IV infusion. Level (target 10–20 mcg/mL total / 1–2 mcg/mL free). LFTs.
drugs.adverse_effects: Cardiac arrhythmia / bradycardia / hypotension during infusion (purple-glove syndrome with extravasation), gingival hyperplasia, hepatotoxicity, blood dyscrasias, hypersensitivity (DRESS, SJS).
drugs.contraindications: HLA-B*15:02 carrier (SJS risk); severe hepatic impairment; hypersensitivity. Concomitant delavirdine.
drugs.black_box_warning: IV infusion rate ≤ 1–3 mg/kg/min — severe hypotension and arrhythmia at higher rates. Continuous cardiac monitoring required.
drugs.incompatibilities: Will precipitate in dextrose-containing solutions. Use NS only. Flush IV line before and after.

review.notes: Fosphenytoin (phenytoin pro-drug, dosed as PE) is gentler on tissue but expensive — Neofax page 431.

drugs.monitoring: Clinical response; CBC, RFT, electrolytes, LFTs.
drugs.adverse_effects: Diarrhoea, eosinophilia, transaminitis, neutropenia (especially > 7 days), thrombocytosis, AKI (especially with concurrent vancomycin).

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: HCO₃ needed (mEq) = base deficit (mEq/L) × 0.3 × wt (kg) — give half, reassess

doses.indication: Resuscitation (routine use NOT recommended in NRP)
doses.comments: Establish ventilation FIRST. Routine bicarbonate in cardiac arrest is not recommended.

drugs.administration: Use 4.2 % (0.5 mEq/mL) for neonates — NEVER 8.4 % undiluted. Slow infusion to avoid rapid pH shifts → IVH.
drugs.monitoring: Blood gas, electrolytes (Na, K), BP.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypernatraemia, hyperosmolarity, IVH (preterm — rapid push), tissue necrosis on extravasation, paradoxical CSF acidosis.
drugs.contraindications: Hypoventilation (respiratory acidosis without ventilation support), metabolic alkalosis, hypocalcaemia.
drugs.incompatibilities: Do NOT mix with calcium, catecholamines (adrenaline / noradrenaline / dopamine — inactivated).

review.notes: Always document salt strength: 4.2 % = 0.5 mEq/mL, 8.4 % = 1 mEq/mL.

doses.indication: MRSA / coagulase-neg staph / penicillin-resistant strep sepsis

doses.frequency: every 8–48 hours by PMA, weight, and creatinine

drugs.monitoring: Therapeutic levels — Trough (just pre-next-dose) 10–20 mg/L (some target AUC₂₄/MIC ≥ 400). Renal function (Cr, urine output) every 2–3 days. Audiology screen if prolonged or with concurrent ototoxic drugs.
drugs.adverse_effects: Nephrotoxicity (especially with concurrent piperacillin-tazobactam, aminoglycosides, contrast), 'red-man syndrome' (rapid infusion — flushing, hypotension), ototoxicity (rare), thrombocytopenia, neutropenia.
drugs.renal_adjustment: Extend interval; redose by trough levels.
drugs.incompatibilities: Many — use dedicated line where possible.

review.notes: AUC-guided dosing is replacing trough-only dosing in newer guidelines.

doses.frequency: every 1–2 hours as needed by clinical paralysis
doses.comments: May give as continuous infusion 0.05–0.15 mg/kg/hr.

drugs.monitoring: Train-of-four (where available), ventilator synchrony, eye protection, pressure-area care.
drugs.adverse_effects: Tachycardia (mild), prolonged paralysis with renal/hepatic impairment, hypotension (rare).
drugs.contraindications: Use only by trained clinicians familiar with airway management.
drugs.black_box_warning: Should be administered only by adequately trained individuals familiar with its actions and hazards.

review.notes: Reverse with sugammadex (where available) or neostigmine + atropine.

doses.indication: VKDB prophylaxis — oral alternative when IM refused
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 2 mg PO at birth, then 1 mg/week × 3 months  OR  2 mg PO at birth, 4–6 days, and 4–6 weeks
doses.comments: AVOID oral route in preterm, ill, malabsorption, cholestasis, or maternal anticonvulsants.

doses.route: SC (preferred) or slow IV (over ≥ 1 minute, not exceeding 1 mg/min)
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 1 mg/dose; higher doses (1–10 mg) have been used IV
doses.comments: Avoid IM in known coagulopathy. Anaphylaxis risk with IV — dilute and infuse slowly.

drugs.monitoring: Coagulation (PT/INR) in treatment of VKDB or therapeutic use.
drugs.adverse_effects: Anaphylaxis with IV / IM (rare but reported), pain at injection site, transient hyperbilirubinaemia.
drugs.black_box_warning: Severe hypersensitivity reactions including anaphylaxis with IV / IM use — dilute and infuse slowly. Avoid rapid IV.

review.notes: IM is the preferred route in India and globally; oral schedule is for parental refusal of IM.
